name: Lebanese International University

admissions_process.summary: Graduate applicants apply through the admissions flow, submit the required file, and complete any school- or program-specific steps such as entrance tests or additional review where published.
steps: Review the program and the school-level graduate admissions information.
Prepare and submit the graduate application file with the required documents.
Pay the application fee and complete any entrance-test or interview step if required.
Register and pay tuition according to the published payment instructions.

required_documents.summary: LIU publishes a graduate document checklist that includes academic records, identification, a photo, and supporting admission paperwork; the discovery pass captured the items but not a single consolidated checklist page with stable text for every variant.
items: Certified copy of Lebanese Baccalaureate, Technical Baccalaureate, or equivalent foreign baccalaureate
Certified copy of university degree or transcript, depending on the graduate page variant
National Social Security papers if available, or payment/form processing

language_requirements.summary: LIU does not publish a single university-wide graduate language policy page in the reviewed sources; English proficiency and placement/testing are used, and some programs expect specific language preparation.
notes: The admissions pages and factsheet point to English competence documentation and placement testing.
MBA and engineering program pages reference school-level academic preparation rather than a single graduate language rule.

tuition_model.summary: LIU publishes graduate tuition by school/program on the Tuition Fees page. The frozen inventory maps tuition program-by-program from the current official school rates and program evidence, with all 15 master programs priced.
tuition_model.notes: The official tuition page states that tuition may change without notice. MBA emphases remain grouped under the single MBA record; LIU does not expose separate program pages for each emphasis in the discovery package.

tuition_table.notes: School of Business graduate tuition is published as USD 225 per credit; MBA emphases remain grouped under one MBA record.

tuition_table.official_degree_name: Master of Business Administration with Thesis (MBAT)
tuition_table.notes: MBAT follows the School of Business graduate rate published on the official tuition page.

tuition_table.official_degree_name: WORMS / LIU-Hochschule Worms MBA dual degree
tuition_table.notes: The dual-degree MBA is mapped at the School of Business graduate rate published on the official tuition page.

tuition_table.official_degree_name: MS in Computer and Communications Engineering
tuition_table.notes: Official tuition page lists the School of Engineering graduate rate as USD 285 per credit.
Official tuition page lists the School of Engineering graduate rate as USD 285 per credit.
Official tuition page lists the School of Engineering graduate rate as USD 285 per credit.

Official tuition page lists the School of Engineering graduate rate as USD 285 per credit.
Official tuition page lists the School of Engineering graduate rate as USD 285 per credit.
Official tuition page lists the School of Engineering graduate rate as USD 285 per credit.

tuition_table.official_degree_name: Master of Science in Computer Science
tuition_table.notes: Official tuition page lists the Computer Science graduate rate as USD 285 per credit.

tuition_table.official_degree_name: Master of Science in Food Technology
tuition_table.notes: Official tuition page lists the Food Technology graduate rate as USD 240 per credit.

tuition_table.official_degree_name: Master of Science in Mathematics / Applied Mathematics
tuition_table.notes: Official tuition page lists the MS Applied Math graduate rate as USD 190 per credit; LIU uses dual naming in the official graduate pages.

tuition_table.official_degree_name: Masters of Education in Teaching English as a Second Language
tuition_table.notes: Official tuition page lists the School of Education graduate rate as USD 190 per credit.

application_fee.notes: Graduate application fee published in official admissions materials.

registration_fee.notes: Official tuition page lists a registration fee of USD 150.

notes: Tuition fees are charged per credit.
The tuition page says fee amounts may change without notice.

payment_methods.summary: LIU states that tuition can be paid through FNB and OMT; admissions materials also route the application fee through the admissions process.

payment_plans.summary: The official brochure states that tuition payment may be divided into four payments per semester or eight payments per year.

scholarships.summary: The reviewed official sources do not publish a distinct graduate scholarship catalogue. Financial aid is referenced generally, but the scholarship surface was not separately itemized in this pass.

financial_aid.summary: LIU says financial aid is available generally based on academic achievement and financial need. The tuition page notes that School of Pharmacy students are not eligible for financial aid.
details: Financial aid depends on academic achievement and financial need.
Financial aid forms are available from the Public Relations Office.
School of Pharmacy students are not eligible for financial aid.

academic_calendar.summary: An official academic calendar page was found, but the detailed graduate dates were not fully extracted in this pass.

graduate_regulations.summary: The University Catalogue is the main official source for graduate rules, admissions, transfer criteria, graduation requirements, and school-specific curriculum sections.

accreditation.summary: No distinct university-wide graduate accreditation page was isolated in the reviewed official sources for this pass.

international_students.summary: LIU publishes official exchange and visiting-student material, including mobility guidance, passport-copy expectations, and English competence documentation for international applicants.
items: Exchange and visiting student guidance is published in an official PDF.
International applicants are expected to provide passport-based identification when appropriate.
English competence documentation appears in the factsheet and graduate admissions materials.

language_policy.summary: No single university-wide graduate language policy page was found. The reviewed sources indicate English proficiency/placement expectations and school-level program language variation.

notes: MBA emphases are retained as concentrations under one MBA record rather than split into separate programs.
The inventory keeps 15 master programs and zero PhD records, with PharmD and doctoral research references out of scope.
The university tuition table is program-specific, but every mapped value comes from official LIU tuition sources only.
